devdoc.details: Core multisig wallet implementation used behind ERC1967 constructor proxies

Initialized(uint64).details: Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized.

approveAndExecute(bytes32).details: Returns false (not revert) when timelocked but approved — ThresholdReached event      distinguishes "approved but waiting" from "not enough approvals" for off-chain callers.      TransactionApproved is emitted only when a new approval is recorded (not if already approved).

params.txHash: Transaction hash to approve and potentially execute

returns.executed: Whether the transaction was executed (false = not enough approvals OR timelocked)

cancelByConsensus(bytes32).details: Requires threshold approvals on the cancel proposal itself — equivalent authority      to executing the original transaction. Self-calls bypass the timelock, so cancellation      is always faster than a timelocked external call (critical for emergency response).      Works on both pre-threshold and post-threshold transactions; the access control is the      onlySelf requirement (full quorum), not the target transaction's approval state.      I-1: Previously named cancelApprovedTransaction — renamed for accuracy (works on any      pending tx, not only approved ones).

cancelTransaction(bytes32).details: Proposer can cancel their own proposal only when approvedAt == 0 (quorum never reached).      Once approvedAt is set it is permanent — even if approvers subsequently revoke,      the proposer cannot cancel. This closes the revoke+cancel clock-gaming attack.      Post-threshold cancellation requires cancelByConsensus (onlySelf, full consensus).

domainSeparator().details: I-2: Includes name ("QuaiVault") and version ("1") fields for standard compliance      and wallet UI display (MetaMask, Ledger Live etc. show these in signing dialogs).      Computed fresh each call (not cached) for fork safety.

enableModule(address).details: WARNING: Enabled modules have unrestricted execution access — see execTransactionFromModule.      Only enable audited, trusted modules.

execTransactionFromModule(address,uint25.details: QV-M-2: Delegates to the 4-param version which enforces onlyModule and nonReentrant.      No onlyModule here — msg.sender is unchanged on internal calls, so the 4-param      version's onlyModule check is sufficient and avoids a redundant SLOAD.

Follows Safe's module trust model: enabled modules have unrestricted execution access.      ⚠️ WARNING: Only enable audited, trusted modules. An enabled module can execute arbitrary      transactions including DelegateCall to any target, addOwner, removeOwner, changeThreshold,      enableModule, disableModule, and setMinExecutionDelay. The security gate is exclusively at      enableModule, which requires owner multisig consensus.

execTransactionFromModuleReturnData(addr.details: Same trust model as execTransactionFromModule — unrestricted module access. See above.

executeTransaction(bytes32).details: C-6 Lazy clock: if a timelocked external tx reached threshold without going through      approveTransaction (e.g., threshold was lowered mid-flight), the first call sets      approvedAt and returns — it does NOT revert, because EVM reverts roll back all state      changes, which would undo the approvedAt assignment. Caller must call again after delay.

expireTransaction(bytes32).details: Permissionless — expiration is an immutable on-chain fact; anyone can trigger cleanup.      Not a griefing vector: can only be called after block.timestamp > expiration, at which      point _executeTransaction would also revert with TransactionIsExpired. The tx is already      dead; expireTransaction formalizes it and reclaims the approval storage.      L-3: Sets expiredTxs[txHash]=true so callers can distinguish expired from cancelled      without relying solely on event logs.

getModules().details: M-5: Uses internal _getModulesPaginated — no external this.call.

hasApproved(bytes32,address).details: H-2: Returns false for approvals made before the owner was last removed.      An approval made at version N is invalid after removal (which advances to version N+1).

params._minExecutionDelay: Vault-level minimum timelock for external calls in seconds (0 = simple quorum)

isValidSignature(bytes32,bytes).details: I-3: This implementation uses a mapping-based (pre-approval) model, NOT ECDSA verification.      The `_signature` parameter is intentionally ignored — any bytes value is accepted.      Validity is determined purely by whether signMessage(abi.encode(_dataHash)) was executed      via multisig consensus.      Workflow for integrators:        1. Multisig proposes and executes: wallet.signMessage(abi.encode(messageHash))        2. External protocol calls: wallet.isValidSignature(messageHash, anySig)        3. Returns magic value 0x1626ba7e if pre-approved, 0xffffffff otherwise.      IMPORTANT: Protocols expecting live ECDSA signatures will NOT work without first      pre-approving the message hash via multisig. This is a deliberate architectural choice      to avoid ecrecover complexity and signature malleability risks.The signature parameter (I-5) is intentionally unnamed and ignored — see NatSpec above.

params._dataHash: The hash of the data to validate

returns._0: 0x1626ba7e if message was pre-approved via multisig, 0xffffffff otherwise

params.expiration: Unix timestamp after which tx cannot be executed (0 = no expiry)
Unix timestamp after which tx cannot be executed (0 = no expiry)
params.requestedDelay: Additional delay in seconds beyond vault minimum (0 = use vault floor)

revokeApproval(bytes32).details: Does NOT clear approvedAt — once the timelock clock starts it never resets.      Revocation can prevent execution (by dropping below threshold) but cannot      re-open the proposer's cancelTransaction path once approvedAt is set.

params.disabled: true = block all module DelegateCall operations (secure default);                 false = allow module DelegateCall (required for MultiSend batching)

setMinExecutionDelay(uint32).details: Prospective only — does NOT affect in-flight transactions whose executionDelay      was locked in at proposal time. Requires multisig self-call consensus (`onlySelf`).      Modules with `execTransactionFromModule` access can call this; only enable trusted modules.

params.delay: New minimum delay in seconds (0 = simple quorum, no timelock)

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

DOMAIN_NAME_HASH.details: EIP-712 precomputed domain name hash (avoids runtime keccak256 in domainSeparator)

DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_TYPEHASH.details: EIP-712 domain separator typehash (I-2: includes name and version for standard compliance)

QUAIVAULT_MSG_TYPEHASH.details: EIP-712 message typehash for QuaiVault signed messages

_ERC1967_IMPL_SLOT.details: ERC1967 implementation storage slot (BB-L-4: post-delegatecall invariant check)

_approvalEpochs.details: An approval is valid iff _approvalEpochs[txHash][owner] == ownerVersions[owner] + 1.      Removing an owner increments ownerVersions[owner], automatically invalidating all their      prior approvals without iterating in-flight transactions. If the same address is re-added,      old approvals are still invalidated (new epoch required). Use hasApproved() to query.Overflow note: ownerVersions would need to reach type(uint256).max for the sentinel value 0      to falsely match — requiring 2^256 removals of the same address. Physically impossible.

delegatecallDisabled.details: Defaults to true (security-first). Closes the entire class of delegatecall-based      storage corruption attacks (Bybit vector, arbitrary slot overwrite, SELFDESTRUCT via      delegatecall). Owners can disable via setDelegatecallDisabled(false) if a module      legitimately requires delegatecall (e.g., MultiSend batching).

expiredTxs.details: Set true by expireTransaction(). Cancelled transactions have cancelled=true but      expiredTxs[txHash]=false. Expired transactions have both cancelled=true and      expiredTxs[txHash]=true. Use this mapping for unambiguous struct introspection.

minExecutionDelay.details: Prospective only — changing this does NOT affect in-flight transactions, which      retain the executionDelay locked in at proposal time.

ownerVersions.details: Starts at 0; approval stored as ownerVersions[owner]+1. Incrementing on removal      atomically invalidates all prior approvals from that address without touching in-flight      transaction storage.

devdoc.title: QuaiVault

DelegatecallDisabledChanged(bool).notice: CR-1: Emitted when the delegatecall security flag is toggled

MessageUnsigned(bytes32,bytes).notice: Emitted when a signed message is revoked (M-NEW-3)

MinExecutionDelayChanged(uint32,uint32).notice: Emitted when vault-level minimum delay changes

ThresholdReached(bytes32,uint48,uint256).notice: Emitted once when threshold is first crossed; executableAfter is fixed and final

TransactionExpired(bytes32).notice: Emitted when an expired tx is formally closed via expireTransaction

TransactionFailed(bytes32,address,bytes).notice: Emitted on external call failure (Option B — never reverts on external failures)

TransactionProposed(bytes32,address,addr.notice: I-1: Includes expiration and executionDelay for complete off-chain lifecycle tracking

MAX_EXECUTION_DELAY().notice: Maximum allowed minExecutionDelay (30 days) to prevent accidental wallet bricking (BB-M-1)

MAX_OWNERS().notice: Maximum number of owners allowed (prevents DoS from gas-intensive loops)

approveAndExecute(bytes32).notice: Approve and execute a transaction in one call if threshold is met and timelock elapsed

cancelByConsensus(bytes32).notice: Cancel any pending transaction via multisig consensus (self-call)

cancelTransaction(bytes32).notice: Cancel a pending transaction (C-2: only proposer, only before threshold ever crossed)

delegatecallDisabled().notice: CR-1: When true, modules cannot execute DelegateCall operations.

execTransactionFromModule(address,uint25.notice: Execute transaction from authorized module (Zodiac IAvatar compatible)

execTransactionFromModuleReturnData(addr.notice: Execute transaction from module and return data (Zodiac IAvatar compatible)

executeTransaction(bytes32).notice: Execute a transaction after threshold is met

expireTransaction(bytes32).notice: Formally close an expired transaction and free approval storage

expiredTxs(bytes32).notice: L-3: Distinguishes expired transactions from voluntarily cancelled ones.

getModules().notice: Get all enabled modules (limited to MAX_MODULES)

getModulesPaginated(address,uint256).notice: Get paginated list of enabled modules (Zodiac IAvatar compatible)

getTransactionHash(address,uint256,bytes.notice: Get transaction hash (C-1: uses abi.encode for unambiguous encoding)

hasApproved(bytes32,address).notice: Check if an owner has a valid (current-epoch) approval for a transaction

isOwner(address).notice: Mapping of address to owner status

minExecutionDelay().notice: Vault-level minimum execution delay for external calls in seconds (0 = simple quorum)

moduleCount().notice: Count of enabled modules for MAX_MODULES enforcement (L-3)

nonce().notice: Transaction nonce for unique hash generation

ownerVersions(address).notice: H-2: Per-owner version counter incremented on each removal

proposeTransaction(address,uint256,bytes.notice: Propose a new transaction (backward-compatible overload, no expiry, no delay)
Propose a new transaction with expiration (C-7)
Propose a new transaction with expiration and per-transaction delay

revokeApproval(bytes32).notice: Revoke approval for a pending transaction

setDelegatecallDisabled(bool).notice: CR-1: Toggle the delegatecall security hardening flag

setMinExecutionDelay(uint32).notice: Update vault-level minimum execution delay for external calls

signedMessages(bytes32).notice: Mapping of approved message hashes for EIP-1271 signature validation

threshold().notice: Number of approvals required to execute a transaction

transactions(bytes32).notice: Mapping of transaction hash to transaction data

userdoc.notice: This is the implementation contract used by all proxy instances
